acceptance_targets: [CH11_CRAFT_FOLIO_LOCATION_CANON_BREAK] Pass if a cold reader can read the revised discovery block and state, without outside context, that the bonding folio is housed in the gated archive or an explicitly gated archive subsection, and the chapter no longer implies Hermione retrieved it alone from ordinary Estate Administration shelves while Draco was elsewhere. Fail if the decisive text still appears to come from the main-library lower shelves with no gating explanation.

[CH11_DIALOGUE_EXAMPLE_LINE_COPY] Pass if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:13 is not "Mm." and is not near-verbatim to any Draco example line in the style bible, while still conveying distracted assent before the examination continues. Fail if the line remains "Mm." or simply swaps to another copied example-line fragment.

[CH11_PROSE_DOUBT_RESTATEMENT_LOOP] Pass if, after the folio explanation lands, the revised chapter states the central doubt once, dramatizes one on-page concealment beat with Draco, and ends on the conscious lie without a second full paragraph that merely rephrases that she cannot tell whether the feeling is hers or the house's. Hermione's late-scene interiority must show at least one messy self-correction or aborted rationalization on the page. Fail if both the dinner section and the bedroom coda still stop to restate the same mechanism in abstract summary after the reader already understands it.
[CHAPTER_END_CH11] Pass if the chapter ends on the concealment decision or its immediate consequences without 'did not sleep.' Fail if a bedroom/darkness/insomnia beat still closes the chapter.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can read the revised discovery block and state, without outside context, that the bonding folio is housed in the gated archive or an explicitly gated archive subsection, and the chapter no longer implies Hermione retrieved it alone from ordinary Estate Administration shelves while Draco was elsewhere. Fail if the decisive text still appears to come from the main-library lower shelves with no gating explanation.
findings.problem: Hermione finds the bonding folio on an ungated lower shelf in the main library while Draco is separately working in the archive. The continuity sheet canonically places the bonding folio in the gated archive with the Manor's oldest sensitive texts, so this discovery sequence breaks established location logic and weakens the later archive-access stakes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the discovery sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:125, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:127, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:129 so the bonding text is found in a canon-consistent place. Either make shelf 14-C an explicitly archive-only subsection that is open because both of them are already working under the archive condition, or move the actual retrieval into a joint archive beat and keep the general-library survey only as the clue that points her there. Then align the explanatory follow-through at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:145 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:169 so the archive remains the sensitive-text repository rather than a separate place Draco happened to be using while she found the decisive manual elsewhere.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:13 is not "Mm." and is not near-verbatim to any Draco example line in the style bible, while still conveying distracted assent before the examination continues. Fail if the line remains "Mm." or simply swaps to another copied example-line fragment.
findings.problem: Draco's first line is the exact calibration-only example line "Mm." from the style bible. Those example lines are non-copyable reference material, so reproducing one verbatim damages voice integrity even though the local beat is small.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the assent at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:13 with a different response or nonverbal beat that keeps Draco's distracted, guarded register without reproducing calibration text. The surrounding exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:17, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:19 can stay minimal, but the replacement should sound scene-specific rather than lifted from the voice guide.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after the folio explanation lands, the revised chapter states the central doubt once, dramatizes one on-page concealment beat with Draco, and ends on the conscious lie without a second full paragraph that merely rephrases that she cannot tell whether the feeling is hers or the house's. Hermione's late-scene interiority must show at least one messy self-correction or aborted rationalization on the page. Fail if both the dinner section and the bedroom coda still stop to restate the same mechanism in abstract summary after the reader already understands it.
findings.problem: Once the folio has already explained the Manor's mechanism, the back half keeps re-proving the same thesis in abstract summary: every connection is suspect, she cannot tell what is real, maybe it is the house, maybe it is him, maybe she should verify. The repetition blunts the reveal's force, leans on ambient-house-warmth shorthand, and pushes Hermione's private voice toward tidy diagnostic paraphrase instead of the more fractured, self-correcting interiority the style bible specifies.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and roughen the reiteration blocks at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:177, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:183, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:207, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:227, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:261, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:275. Keep the must-land beats: the folio makes prior tenderness suspect, she lies to Draco over dinner, and she consciously chooses concealment before sleep. Cut or rework the duplicate abstract explanations so at least one late passage carries the doubt through behavior, interruption, or self-correction rather than another full thesis-summary, and replace repeated house-warmth paraphrase with one sharper concrete consequence of living inside the doubt.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the chapter ends on the concealment decision or its immediate consequences without 'did not sleep.' Fail if a bedroom/darkness/insomnia beat still closes the chapter.

findings.problem: Chapter 11 ends with 'she lay in the dark and let the lie stand and she did not sleep' (line 3065). This is the seventh 'did not sleep' ending. The chapter's material — Hermione discovering the bonding folio and choosing concealment — generates genuine forward pressure that the ending neutralizes by routing to the established template.
findings.rewrite_direction: End chapter 11 at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3060-3067 on the decision itself — the concealment choice — rather than on the bedroom aftermath. The chapter's dramatic turn is the decision to withhold; end on the weight of that decision, not on insomnia.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch11's examination scene no longer re-describes the instrument layout, sleeve-rolling, or forearm-on-table steps.
findings.evidence: Ch11 lines 2789-2793: 'She would set out her diagnostic instruments... He would roll up his left sleeve and lay his forearm on the padded surface... he would pick up whatever he was reading'; cf. Ch3, Ch6, Ch8 for prior exam-setup descriptions.
findings.problem: By Ch11, the examination routine has been described from scratch in Ch2, Ch3, Ch6, and Ch8. Ch11 re-teaches the full setup. The paragraph acknowledges it 'had become routine,' then describes it anyway.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the step-by-step setup. Open with the diagnostic finding that is new (the base layer strengthening, the correlation with her contact hours).

findings.acceptance_test: Ch11 no longer uses 'sharp angles' to describe Draco's face.
findings.evidence: Ch1 line 153: 'the sharp angles of his boyhood'; Ch11 line 2859: 'The sharp angles of his face, the jaw she'd watched soften'
findings.problem: Ch11 re-describes Draco's sharp facial angles, a trait first introduced in Ch1. 'Sharp angles' is the same descriptor.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch11 line 2859, replace 'sharp angles of his face' with a detail specific to this moment.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch11 has no more than 2 Mark-pain references, each tied to new information or a character decision.
findings.evidence: Ch11: 5 Mark-pain references plus full exam re-description. Manuscript total: 72 Mark-pain references across 18 chapters. Baseline established Ch2-3.
findings.problem: The Mark's pain state is re-proved in Ch11 without materially new information. The baseline 'the Mark hurts' is established in Ch2-3.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut Mark-pain references in Ch11 that merely re-state the ache without new findings or narrative consequences. Keep max 2 that drive decisions.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch11 has no more than 5 resonance references, each tied to a new observation.
findings.evidence: Ch11: 27 'resonance' mentions. Manuscript total: 121 across all chapters. Established Ch5 line 959.
findings.problem: Ch11 re-proves resonance existence 27 times. The phenomenon is established in Ch5 and its significance clear by Ch7.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce resonance references in Ch11 to max 5, each advancing understanding or driving plot.

findings.problem: Em-dashes in 45.7% of prose lines vs 10% threshold. ~52 lines need conversion.
findings.rewrite_direction: Convert ~52 em-dash lines to colons, semicolons, periods, or restructured sentences. Retain em-dashes only for dramatic interruption.

findings.problem: 'She' opens 22.2% of sentences vs 7% threshold. ~59 need restructuring.
findings.rewrite_direction: Restructure ~59 She-opened sentences using subordinate clauses, prepositional phrases, gerunds, or sentence combining.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel is dark, intense, and emotionally devastating. The premise asks for a love story built inside trauma, in a house that may be manipulating its inhabitants, between two people marked by the same war in different ways. The creative risk is not in the darkness — it is in the tenderness. The hardest thing this novel must do is make the quiet moments as fully rendered as the painful ones, and make the desire as specific as the grief. Do not retreat from the body: the Mark examinations are physical events with physical consequences. The kiss is a physical event. The scar is a physical object on a real arm. Do not retreat from the doubt: the Manor's possible manipulation is not a problem to be solved in act three but a condition to be lived inside for the entire novel. Do not retreat from the ugliness of the drawing room scene or the specificity of what Bellatrix did. And do not retreat from the gentleness of the domestic scenes — tenderness between damaged people is harder to write than pain and should be given equal craft attention.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to bad prose. When the scene calls for specificity — in pain, in desire, in shame, in violence, in grief — render it. The drawing room scene must feel like being in the room. The Mark examinations must feel like touch. The scar reveal must feel like exposure. Fading to implication at the moment of consequence wastes every scene that built to that moment.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Narcissa. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Narcissa's default register. An unjustified uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be flagged and revised.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or domestic ones. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters are allowed for compression; three-scene chapters should not all follow the same internal rhythm. Vary dialogue density — some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are predominantly interior. Never give two consecutive chapters the same structural shape. A chapter that builds to a confrontation should not be followed by another chapter that builds to a confrontation; a quiet aftermath chapter should not be followed by another quiet aftermath chapter. The rhythm of the book as a whole should feel like breathing — expansion and contraction, tension and release, pressure and air.
prose_style_profile.diction: Literary but not ornate. The prose should feel crafted without feeling written — precise where precision matters, rough where roughness is true. Avoid both the polished-prestige-novel voice and the genre-fiction utility voice. The diction should sound like someone telling a story who is very good at telling stories but not performing the act of telling.
forbidden_drift_patterns: The metaphor chain — a comparison that extends across three or more sentences and becomes the scene's organizing image rather than a local effect
Emotional stage directions — 'she felt a surge of' or 'something tightened in his chest' or 'a cold knot formed' as substitutes for specific rendered emotion
The retrospective frame — 'she would later realize' or 'he didn't know then' or other future-looking narrative commentary that drains present-tense urgency
Symmetrical sentence pairs — 'She was X. He was Y.' as a repeated structural pattern, especially in dual-POV transitions
The ambient-magic-as-mood fallback — using the Manor's temperature or the Mark's ache to externalize emotional states that should be rendered through behavior and thought
Catalogue of sensory impressions used as scene-setting rather than scene-building — lists of what a room looks, smells, sounds like that delay rather than create dramatic pressure
The literary-fiction semicolon habit — using semicolons to connect observations that would land harder as separate sentences or would reveal more if left unconnected
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating by chapter. Hermione's chapters are in her interiority — analytical, self-aware, faster. Draco's chapters are in his — more guarded, more sensory, the observations sharper and the self-knowledge harder-won. The POV character's biases should be legible without being announced.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable. Default to medium-length sentences with occasional long, accumulating ones and occasional short declaratives for force. Avoid sustained staccato. Avoid sustained flowing prose. The rhythm should shift with scene pressure — tighter under tension, more expansive in quiet and reflective passages.
sensory_bias: tactile — the Mark examinations, the Manor's surfaces, temperature, skin contact, the physical experience of proximity
architectural and spatial — the Manor as sensory environment, corridors, rooms, light, the way space itself communicates
proprioceptive — body awareness, the resonance between them as a felt physical phenomenon, pain and pleasure as data
